content:
After nearly a decade of writing C and C++ for embedded devices, building Yocto distros, and debugging firmware over UART at odd hours — I figured it’s time to start writing some of this down.
This blog is where I’ll share what I learn, mostly from real projects:
Yocto — custom distros, BSP layers, KAS builds, OTA updates with RAUC and SWUpdate Zephyr RTOS — firmware for nRF, ESP32, OpenThread, BLE, MCUboot DFU Embedded Linux — kernel config, device tree, systemd, security hardening Edge & IoT — gateways, wireless protocols, containers on embedded, edge AI One article a week. Straight from the build directory.
Stay tuned.
summary: After years of building embedded systems behind closed doors, I’m starting to write about it. Here’s what to expect.
title: Building in Public: Why I'm Starting This Blog

content:
Hey, I’m Umair Embedded software engineer based in Germany, building things at the intersection of Linux, wireless protocols, and edge computing.
I write C and C++ for resource-constrained devices, build custom Linux distributions with Yocto, and work on firmware for Zephyr-based IoT devices. I’ve done this across smart grid, automotive, energy automation, retail IoT, and lighting.
This blog is where I share what I learn — mostly about Yocto, Zephyr, embedded Linux, and OTA updates. Everything here comes from real projects and real hardware.
Get in touch GitHub LinkedIn
title: About
